name: Toby
manager_notes: Toby has strong execution and team trust, but after being passed over last cycle he has become cautious about taking visible strategic positions. He keeps shipping, yet he is not consistently converting design leadership into org-level influence. I need this conversation to clarify whether the gap is confidence, sponsorship, or unclear expectations at the next level. He should leave with a concrete growth contract tied to decision ownership and cross-functional impact, not a generic development list.
answers: I am still motivated, but the promotion outcome made me more careful about where I spend political capital.
I can lead major initiatives, but I do not always translate that into visible strategic influence.
I get positive feedback on craft and collaboration, but less clarity on what separates me at the next level.
Sometimes I avoid high-ambiguity forums because I do not want to sound underprepared.
I think I need to own a cross-org decision area, not just deliver strong design outcomes.
I also need clearer sponsorship in rooms where priorities are set.
If we define two concrete leadership bets for this half, I can commit fully.
I want milestones that show progress before the next cycle, not only retrospective feedback.
I want to start with one leadership bet where I drive the decision, not just the design output.
